meaning: n.一种脆饼干,<美俚>[音] 法国号
examples: When you hear the word "yoga", do you think of a person with his legs twisted up like a pretzel?
All we know is that in 610 A.D., a monk in northern Italy first twisted the pretzel into its unusual shape.
The first citation for pretzel in English comes in the 1820s in a collection called Antiquities of Long Island.
Investigator were aided by an unlikely clue -- a pretzel bag visible in one video.
And for all its fevered, pretzel-logic stridulations, it fails to make any case for the latter.
